title: marcgraviaceae
intro: The rank Marcgraviaceae refers to a family of flowering plants. It is a group of plants that includes about 50 genera and 900 species, primarily found in tropical regions of Central and South America, as well as Africa. Members of the Marcgraviaceae family are known for their unique flowers, which often have a cup-shaped or funnel-shaped structure. These plants typically have simple or compound leaves and can be found growing in a variety of habitats, including rainforests and cloud forests.
